humanities_role_explanation: Uses narrative passages from novels as content for the word prediction task, but the benchmark is primarily designed for evaluating language modeling capabilities
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only benchmark consisting of narrative passages with context sentences and a target sentence where the last word must be predicted
analytical_tasks_explanation: Tasks require predicting words that can only be guessed with broad discourse context, involving co-reference resolution, inference of prototypical participants in events, and understanding narrative continuations

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specialized benchmark focused specifically on word prediction requiring broad discourse context

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates traditional language models (RNN, LSTM, N-Gram, Memory Networks) from 2016, predating modern LLMs

llm_is_primary_system.note: Tests traditional language models, not large language models

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics - uses narrative passages from novels for discourse understanding tasks

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy, perplexity, and median rank of correct word

summary: LAMBADA introduces a word prediction benchmark requiring broad discourse context understanding, where target words can be guessed by humans when given full passages but not from local sentence context alone. The dataset consists of 10,022 narrative passages from novels, filtered through human annotation to ensure broad context dependency.
key_findings: All tested language models performed poorly on LAMBADA (≤1% accuracy) compared to 19-21% on control data, with the best model being N-Gram with cache. 80% of LAMBADA items have target words appearing in context, with proper nouns comprising 48% of targets.
paper_name: Evaluating Automatic Speech Recognition Systems on Multilingual Audio Benchmarks
